---
title: "A geostatistical analysis of the equitability of the distribution of municipal trees in Copenhagen"
author: "Christian K. Kjeldsen"
date: "r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')"
output:
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: kate
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(rmdformats)
library(tmap)

## Global options
options(max.print="75")
opts_chunk$set(echo=TRUE,
               cache=TRUE,
               prompt=FALSE,
               tidy=TRUE,
               comment=NA,
               message=FALSE,
               warning=FALSE)
opts_knit$set(width=75)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# Use static maps for knitting instead of interactive:
tmap_mode("plot")
```


```{r libraries, include=FALSE}
# load needed libraries
library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(spatstat)
library(sfarrow)
library(mapview)
```

```{r load data, cache=TRUE}
# Load districts
districts <- st_read("data/districts.json")
# Test they look right
# plot(districts)

# Test data with 500 trees is provided in the data/ folder. 
# The test dataset can be loaded using the line below, removing the '#':
#trees <- st_read("data/trees_500_test_data.json")
# If this is done, do not run lines 53-54.

# Load trees from URL. It takes some time due to the large size of this dataset. 
URL <- "https://wfs-kbhkort.kk.dk/k101/ows?service=WFS&version=1.0.0&request=GetFeature&typeName=k101:trae_basis&outputFormat=application%2Fjson&SRSNAME=EPSG:4326&Maxfeatures=100000"
trees <- st_read(URL)
```


```{r check data numbers}
# Check number of districs and trees
cat("Districts:", nrow(districts), "\n")
cat("Trees:", nrow(trees))
```


```{r clean tree data and set WGS84}
# Keep only location in tree dataset, dropping all unused columns
trees <- trees %>% select(geometry)

# Make sure both layers are WGS84 (EPSG:4326) 
districts <- st_transform(districts, 4326)
trees <- st_transform(trees, 4326)
```

```{r visual check of overlap, eval=FALSE}
# Check that trees and districts overlap correctly now
mapview(districts) + mapview(trees, cex = 1, col.regions = "forestgreen")
```


```{r project to UTM 32N and check overlap}
# Project both to UTM 32N (EPSG:25832)
districts_utm <- st_transform(districts, 25832)
trees_utm <- st_transform(trees, 25832)

# A non-visual check that the trees are within the districts:
trees_utm <- trees_utm[st_intersects(trees_utm,
                                      st_union(districts_utm),
                                      sparse = FALSE)[,1], ]
cat("Trees within districts:", nrow(trees_utm), "\n")
```

```{r tree densities per district}
# Tree counts per district and tree density, in trees per square km
districts_utm <- districts_utm %>%
  mutate(tree_count = lengths(st_contains(districts_utm, trees_utm)),
    area_km2 = as.numeric(st_area(districts_utm)) / 1e6,
    tree_density = tree_count / area_km2)

cat("\nTree counts per district:\n")
print(districts_utm %>% st_drop_geometry() %>%
        select(navn, tree_count, area_km2, tree_density) %>%
        arrange(desc(tree_density)))

```

```{r tree density per 500m hex grid}
# Trees per 500 m  hex grid
hex_grid <- st_make_grid(districts_utm, cellsize = 500, square = FALSE) %>%
  st_sf() %>%
  st_intersection(st_union(districts_utm)) %>%
  mutate(hex_id = row_number(),
    tree_count = lengths(st_contains(., trees_utm)),
    area_km2 = as.numeric(st_area(.)) / 1e6,
    tree_density = tree_count / area_km2)
```


```{r tree density per square 250m grid}
# Trees per 250x250m grid
sq_grid <- st_make_grid(districts_utm, cellsize = 250, square = TRUE) %>%
  st_sf() %>%
  st_intersection(st_union(districts_utm)) %>%
  mutate(cell_id = row_number(),
    tree_count = lengths(st_contains(., trees_utm)),
    area_km2 = as.numeric(st_area(.)) / 1e6,
    tree_density = tree_count / area_km2)
```


```{r map the tree densities per district, hex grid, and square grid}
# Make maps of trees per district, hex grid, and square grid
make_density_map <- function(sf_obj, density_col, title) {
  tm_basemap("Esri.WorldGrayCanvas") +
  tm_shape(sf_obj %>% filter(.data[[density_col]] > 0)) +
    tm_polygons(
      fill = density_col,
      fill.scale  = tm_scale_intervals(style  = "quantile", n = 6, values = rev(hcl.colors(6, "YlGn"))), fill.legend = tm_legend(title = "Trees / km²"), fill_alpha = 0.8, col = "grey", lwd = 0.3) + tm_layout(title = title, legend.outside = TRUE)}

# Make the three map objects
map_districts <- make_density_map(districts_utm, "tree_density", "Tree density – Districts")

map_hex <- make_density_map(hex_grid, "tree_density", "Tree density – 500 m hex grid")

map_square <- make_density_map(sq_grid, "tree_density", "Tree density – 250 m square grid")

# And save them to the output/ folder 
tmap_save(tm = map_districts, filename = "C:/Users/ckjel.CK_THINKPAD/Desktop/CDS_Spatial/cds-spatial/Exam/output/map_districts.png", dpi = 300)
tmap_save(tm = map_hex, filename = "C:/Users/ckjel.CK_THINKPAD/Desktop/CDS_Spatial/cds-spatial/Exam/output/map_hex.png", dpi = 300)
tmap_save(tm = map_square, filename = "C:/Users/ckjel.CK_THINKPAD/Desktop/CDS_Spatial/cds-spatial/Exam/output/map_square.png", dpi = 300)

```


```{r combined tree density map}
# Make a map showing the three side-by-side. 

# Helper function to force legend to be visible in combined plot
force_legend_right <- function(map) {map + tm_layout(legend.outside = FALSE, legend.position = c("right", "bottom"), legend.text.size = 1, legend.title.size = 1.5, title.size = 2.5)}

# Side-by-side view of the three maps:
combined_tree_density_map <- tmap_arrange(
  force_legend_right(map_districts),
  force_legend_right(map_hex),
  force_legend_right(map_square),
  ncol = 3)

# Save to the output/ folder:
tmap_save(tm = combined_tree_density_map, filename = "C:/Users/ckjel.CK_THINKPAD/Desktop/CDS_Spatial/cds-spatial/Exam/output/combined_tree_density_map.png", width = 24, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "in")
```


```{r PPP}
# Make observation window from union of districts
city_boundary <- st_union(districts_utm)
city_window <- as.owin(city_boundary)

# Get coordinates from trees
tree_coords <- st_coordinates(trees_utm)
trees_ppp <- ppp(x = tree_coords[, "X"], y = tree_coords[, "Y"], window = city_window)
```


```{r quadrat test}
# Divide area into 5×5 quadrats and test against complete spatial randomness, CSR
qt <- quadrat.test(trees_ppp, nx = 5)
print(qt)
# shows that df = 19, p-value < 2.2e-16, that is p<<0.05, meaning we reject CSR 
plot(qt, main = "Quadrat test – Copenhagen trees")

# Try with smaller quadrats for robustness
qt_1 <- quadrat.test(trees_ppp, nx = 10)
print(qt_1)
```


```{r nearest-neighbor distance and G function, cache=TRUE, dependson="PPP"}
# Find mean nearest neighbor distance
mean_nnd <- mean(nndist(trees_ppp))
cat("Mean nearest-neighbour distance:", mean_nnd, "m\n")
# shows a mean nnd of 6.8 meters

# G function with simulation envelope
env_g <- envelope(trees_ppp, Gest, nsim = 99)

plot(env_g, main = "G function - Copenhagen trees")
# this shows that G is above the predicted envelope, which suggests that trees are clustered
```


```{r K function, cache=TRUE, dependson="PPP"}
# K function with simulation envelope and border correction
env_k <- envelope(trees_ppp, Kest, nsim = 99, correction = "border")

plot(env_k, . - theo ~ r, main = "Linearised K function — Copenhagen trees", ylab = "K(r) - pi*r^2")
# The line is above the zero line, meaning that there is more clustering than CSR at scale r (in meters). Trees are most clustered at the scale of the peak value relative to CSR, at approximately 650 m. We see however that trees are clustered at all scales. 
```


```{r BBR as proxy for population}
# Introduce the BBR dataset as a proxy for population
# load needed library
library(jsonlite)

# load BBR data
bbr_cph_raw <- fromJSON(
  "https://sciencedata.dk/public/67e2ad2ca642562dacfa6fdf672a1009/clean_BBR_cph_data.json")

# Flatten it to a data frame and changing the name of a column to get rid of Danish letters
flatten <- function(json) {
  json <- as.data.frame(json[["BygningList"]])
  names(json)[names(json) == "byg026Opførelsesår"] <- "byg026Year"
  return(json)}

# Filter away industrial and agricultural buildings
clean_bbr_df <- function(df, year_cutoff) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(byg404Koordinat)) %>%
    filter(byg404Koordinat != "POINT(0 0)") %>%
    filter(byg406Koordinatsystem == "5") %>%
    filter(!is.na(byg026Year)) %>%
    filter(byg021BygningensAnvendelse %in%
             c("101","120","121","122","131","132","140","150","160","190",
               "321","322","324","331","332","333","334","339",
               "421","422","429","431","432","433","439","441",
               "532","533")) %>%
    distinct(id_lokalId, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    filter(byg026Year >= year_cutoff)}

bbr_cph <- bbr_cph_raw %>%
  flatten() %>%
  clean_bbr_df(year_cutoff = 1900) %>%
  mutate(city = "Copenhagen") %>%
  st_as_sf(wkt = "byg404Koordinat", crs = 25832)

# Residential codes from BBRteknik
residential_codes <- c("101","120","121","122","131","132","140","150","160","190")

# Only keep those buildings which have residential codes 
bbr_cph <- bbr_cph %>%
  mutate(
    building_type = if_else(
      byg021BygningensAnvendelse %in% residential_codes,
      "residential",
      "non_residential"))

# Join residential buildings to their districts
districts_utm_res <- districts_utm %>%
  mutate(residential_count  = lengths(st_contains(districts_utm, bbr_cph %>% filter(building_type == "residential"))))
```


```{r find number of trees per residential building}
# Trees per residential building 
districts_utm_res <- districts_utm_res %>%
  mutate(trees_per_res_building = tree_count / pmax(residential_count, 1))
 
trees_per_res <- districts_utm_res %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(navn, tree_count, residential_count, trees_per_res_building, tree_density) %>%
  arrange(desc(trees_per_res_building))
 
# Show trees per residential building in console
print(trees_per_res)
```

```{r show trees per residential building on map}
# Make a map with trees per residential building shown in colourscale 
trees_per_res_building_map <- tm_basemap("Esri.WorldGrayCanvas") +
  tm_shape(districts_utm_res) + tm_polygons( fill = "trees_per_res_building", fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(breaks = seq(0, 10, by = 1), values = rev(hcl.colors(20, "YlGn"))), fill.legend = tm_legend(title = "Trees / residential building"), col = "grey40", lwd = 0.8, fill_alpha = 0.7) + tm_text("navn", size = 0.6)

# Save to output folder
tmap_save(tm = trees_per_res_building_map, filename = "C:/Users/ckjel.CK_THINKPAD/Desktop/CDS_Spatial/cds-spatial/Exam/output/trees_per_res_building_map.png", dpi = 300)
```


```{r find ratio between the least-served and most-served districts}
# Find the ratio between the least-served and most-served districts
districts_utm_res %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(navn, trees_per_res_building) %>%
  arrange(desc(trees_per_res_building)) %>%
  slice(c(1, n())) %>%
  mutate(ratio = first(trees_per_res_building) / last(trees_per_res_building))
# This shows that Indre By has 11 times more trees per residential building than Brønshøj-Husum.
```


```{r correlation}
# Define 'income' dataframe from "Status på København 2025" report:
income <- data.frame(navn = c("Indre By", "Østerbro", "Nørrebro", "Vesterbro-Kongens Enghave", "Valby", "Vanløse", "Brønshøj-Husum", "Bispebjerg", "Amager Øst", "Amager Vest"), mean_income = c(362, 316, 242, 288, 272, 291, 270, 236, 276, 285))

# Join it to the trees per residential building dataframe:
trees_per_res <- trees_per_res %>% left_join(income, by = "navn")

# Do a Spearman correlation test:
cor.test(trees_per_res$trees_per_res_building, 
         trees_per_res$mean_income, 
         method = "spearman")
```
